steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The function $f$ has the values $f(0)=4$, $f(8)=0$, and $f(20)=0$. The question asks which statement cannot be true — so look for the one that directly contradicts a given value.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $f(20) \lt 0$: The given value $f(20)=0$ means $f(20)$ is not less than zero. ✗
  • Choice B — $f(2) \lt 0$: The given values do not rule this out. ✓
  • Choice C — $f(2) \gt 0$: Similarly possible. ✓
  • Choice D — $f(0) \lt 20$: $f(0) = 4$, and $4 \lt 20$, so this is true. ✓
That matches Choice A — $f(20) \lt 0$.
